item.description:
### Changed - Codex approvals are clearer about what's happening. When Codex asks to run a tool and Perch is waiting on your Allow/Deny, Codex now shows "Waiting for approval in Perch — Codex will prompt automatically if unanswered," so a pause reads as intentional rather than a freeze. If you don't answer in Perch, Codex falls back to its own approval prompt as before. - Tapping a Codex permission notification now jumps to the right place — the Codex desktop app or the exact terminal pane — and hands the decision back to Codex's own prompt, instead of sometimes only revealing the project folder.
### Note - This release updates Perch's Codex hook, so Codex will ask you to approve it again. Run `/hooks` in Codex and trust Perch's entry — the menu bar will remind you if anything isn't trusted.
